findings.title: AI Code Analysis Complete
findings.description: Groq analyzed your page HTML and generated improvement recommendations. See the fix plan below.

summary: The webpage appears to be visually correct and functional, with no obvious issues found. The page loads properly on both desktop and mobile devices, displaying the expected content without any layout or accessibility problems.
bundled_fix_prompt: Fix the web app by modifying the HTML and CSS to improve accessibility and responsiveness. First, add an ID to the div element containing the main content, such as "main-content", and update the CSS to target this ID instead of the generic div element. Update the CSS to include media queries for desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) screen sizes. For desktop, set the font size of the h1 element to 2.5em and the line height to 1.2. For mobile, set the font size of the h1 element to 1.8em and the line height to 1.1. Additionally, update the a:link and a:visited CSS to include a hover effect, such as changing the text color to #666. Ensure the meta viewport tag is set to content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no" to prevent zooming on mobile devices. Update the nav_links, buttons, and forms arrays in the crawl data to include the newly added link with the text "Learn more". Finally, add alt text to any images that may be added in the future, and consider adding ARIA attributes to the a element to improve screen reader support.
